mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and risk appetite over the next month. When FEDFUNDS trends down from its recent level, S&P 500 forward returns should be positive if the move reflects policy easing rather than recession stress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 5.24% in 2006-07, indicating a still-restrictive policy level
failure_condition: Reject if sustained FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the rolling 3-year rank IC stays below 0.03 during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a mild deterioration threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces expected corporate revenues and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The effect should intensify when unemployment is rising from already low levels because the market re-prices growth risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE rises are not associated with negative 20d SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening labor income and retail demand, which supports near-term earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns. The signal is strongest when both series improve together; either series alone is insufficient to confirm the demand impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvements do not outperform single-series signals, or if triggered periods produce nonpositive 20d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals weaker industrial output and margin pressure from slowing real activity, which should weigh on equities over a 60-day horizon. Because industrial production is a broad supply-side activity proxy, persistent monthly contraction should precede negative S&P 500 forward returns when it is not offset by clear monetary easing.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in a majority of occurrences, or if the sign of the conditional return is indistinguishable from zero over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST above recent trend signals demand overheat and tightening affordability, which eventually pressures consumer-sensitive equities through higher rates and weaker discretionary demand. The short-run effect is negative for SPX because housing starts are a leading cyclical demand indicator and a spike often precedes macro tightening.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 8% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST spikes are followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the event-study average return is not below zero after controlling for market direction.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS is below the inflation trend, real policy is loose and equities benefit from easier financial conditions. The signal is positive only when inflation acceleration does not coincide with an aggressive tightening response, because then higher nominal growth supports revenues without fully compressing valuations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate rises above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up / policy-flat episodes do not deliver positive 60d SPX returns on average, or if the interaction term between inflation change and policy stance has no positive predictive value in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS confirms labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and corporate sales prospects, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism should work best when payroll losses are broad and persistent rather than one-month noise.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous weakening in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies a broad final-demand slowdown that should lower earnings expectations and reduce S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is negative because both real output and retail demand must deteriorate together to confirm a genuine demand contraction rather than a sector-specific fluctuation.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction signal is weaker than either single-series trend over rolling out-of-sample windows.
